gaps.description: Binding SSE-capture paths MISS_SSE/HIT_SSE hardcode the authoring session's session-id (.agent_context/sessions/1781106672-6469-2465b2ba685c/...), which is foreign to the Subtask 10/11 consumer sessions that copy this runbook verbatim.
gaps.fix_hint: Derive MISS_SSE/HIT_SSE from a consumer-set RUN_DIR or worktree-relative scratch path; embed no literal session-id. The curl redirect (`> "$MISS_SSE"`) and the python comparator (lines 119-151) both depend on these paths resolving in the consumer's own session. should-be-issue

gaps.description: The force-MISS procedure keys exclusively on the NULLable query_text column, but the report states no precondition that the running proxy build populates query_text on write. Migration 0004 adds query_text as nullable with rationale 'old code emits no value'; against a non-writing build the DELETE matches zero rows, rows_after_delete=0 reports a false-clean, and a stale cached row survives -> consumer mis-asserts freshness.
gaps.fix_hint: Add a one-line precondition that the proxy build must populate query_text on write, plus a sanity check (confirm a freshly written row has non-NULL query_text) before trusting rows_after_delete=0 as a force-MISS proof. should-be-issue

gaps.description: The single verbatim MISS/HIT comparator asserts miss[3]==hit[3] over a fixed turn_classification key set and SHA equality, but the report presents one procedure for all V-rows and does not scope this equality assertion to the cacheable product-search rows (V2-V5) for which it was validated. A takeover row (V7-V20) that unexpectedly produces a DB row would be run through a comparator whose assertion semantics were only proven for the product-search payload shape.
gaps.fix_hint: Explicitly scope the SHA/turn_classification equality assertion to V2-V5; state what a consumer should assert for a takeover row that unexpectedly yields a HIT row.
